title: Terminal Tools
description: JSON Schema definitions for terminal and process management tools available to the ideas-brillantes assistant. Enables shell command execution, script running, process inspection, and environment variable access.

tools.name: exec_command
tools.description: Execute a shell command and return its stdout, stderr, and exit code.

cwd.description: Optional working directory for the command. Defaults to the assistant's current working directory.

timeout.description: Optional timeout in seconds. The process is killed if it exceeds this duration. Defaults to 30 seconds.

tools.name: run_script
tools.description: Execute a multi-line script using a specified interpreter.

script.description: The script source code to execute.

args.description: Optional list of positional arguments to pass to the script (accessible as $1, $2, … or sys.argv[1], …).

tools.name: list_processes
tools.description: List currently running processes, optionally filtered by name or pattern.

filter.description: Optional name substring or regex pattern to filter process list results.

tools.name: kill_process
tools.description: Send a signal to a running process, typically to terminate it.

pid.description: Process ID of the target process.

signal.description: Signal name to send (e.g. 'SIGTERM', 'SIGKILL', 'SIGHUP'). Defaults to 'SIGTERM'.

tools.name: get_env
tools.description: Retrieve the value of one or all environment variables in the current session.

key.description: Name of the environment variable to retrieve (e.g. 'PATH', 'HOME'). If omitted, all environment variables are returned as a key-value map.
